stem: A security system sounds an alarm (Q) when a door sensor (A) is open AND the system is armed (B), OR when a panic button (C) is pressed.
model_answer: The diagram should have three labelled inputs (A, B, C) and one labelled output (Q). An AND gate takes A and B; its output feeds, together with C, into an OR gate whose output is Q. Equivalent expression: Q = (A AND B) OR C.
review_notes: Image variant — pupil draws freehand on the canvas and the PNG is sent to teacher review. Assess correct gate shapes, labelled inputs/output, and the Q = (A AND B) OR C structure.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Draw a logic circuit that produces the output Q described above. Label all three inputs (A, B, C), the gates used, and the output Q.

mark_points.text: three inputs labelled A, B and C, and an output labelled Q
an AND gate with inputs A and B
an OR gate whose inputs are (A AND B) and C
the OR gate output is connected to Q with no redundant gates

misconceptions.label: Swapping AND and OR
misconceptions.description: Q = (A OR B) AND C would only fire when C is pressed as well, not when just the door+armed condition holds.
